an8.30:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Bhaggas, at Suṁsumāragira in the Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park.
an8.30:1.10: this *dhamma* is for one with discernment [*paññavato*], not this *dhamma* for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*].”
an8.30:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Anuruddha was dwelling among the Cedis, in the Eastern Bamboo Park.
an8.30:1.3: Then, while Venerable Anuruddha was alone in seclusion, this reflection arose in his mind [*cetasā*]:
an8.30:1.4: “This *dhamma* is for one of few wishes, not this *dhamma* for one of many wishes;
an8.30:1.5: this *dhamma* is for one who is content, not this *dhamma* for one who is discontent;
an8.30:1.6: this *dhamma* is for one who is secluded, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in company;
an8.30:1.7: this *dhamma* is for one whose energy is aroused, not this *dhamma* for one who is lazy;
an8.30:1.8: this *dhamma* is for one with *sati* established, not this *dhamma* for one with *sati* lost;
an8.30:1.9: this *dhamma* is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not this *dhamma* for one without *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:10.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], and you become one who gains at will, gains without trouble, gains without difficulty these four *jhānas* that are higher mind [*ābhicetasikānaṁ*], dwellings in *sukha* in the present life, then for you, Anuruddha, just as for a householder or a householder’s son there is an upper-story mansion, plastered inside and out, sheltered from the wind, with fastened shutters and closed windows;
an8.30:10.2: so too your lodging at the root of a tree will seem, as you dwell content, for delight, for non-agitation, for easeful dwelling, for descent into *nibbāna*.
an8.30:11.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], and you become one who gains at will, gains without trouble, gains without difficulty these four *jhānas* that are higher mind [*ābhicetasikānaṁ*], dwellings in *sukha* in the present life, then for you, Anuruddha, just as for a householder or a householder’s son there is a couch spread with a woolen cover, spread with a white blanket, spread with a coverlet, with a fine antelope-hide spread, with a canopy above, and red cushions at both ends;
an8.30:11.2: so too your bed and seat of grass bedding will seem, as you dwell content, for delight, for non-agitation, for easeful dwelling, for descent into *nibbāna*.
an8.30:12.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], and you become one who gains at will, gains without trouble, gains without difficulty these four *jhānas* that are higher mind [*ābhicetasikānaṁ*], dwellings in *sukha* in the present life, then for you, Anuruddha, just as for a householder or a householder’s son there are various medicines, namely ghee, fresh butter, oil, honey, and molasses;
an8.30:12.2: so too your medicine of fermented urine will seem, as you dwell content, for delight, for non-agitation, for easeful dwelling, for descent into *nibbāna*.
an8.30:12.3: Therefore, Anuruddha, you should dwell for the next rains residence right here among the Cedis, in the Eastern Bamboo Park.”
an8.30:13.1: Then, having advised Venerable Anuruddha with this advice — just as a strong man might extend his bent arm or bend his extended arm; so too — the *Bhagavā* disappeared from among the Cedis in the Eastern Bamboo Park and appeared among the Bhaggas, at Suṁsumāragira in the Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park.
an8.30:13.10: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with *sati* established, not this *dhamma* for one with *sati* lost;
an8.30:13.11: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not this *dhamma* for one without *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:13.12: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with discernment [*paññavato*], not this *dhamma* for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*];
an8.30:13.13: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who is pleased by non-proliferation, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in proliferation, who is pleased by proliferation.
an8.30:13.2: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat prepared.
an8.30:13.3: Having sat down, the *Bhagavā* addressed the *bhikkhus*:
an8.30:13.4: “I will teach you, *bhikkhus*, the eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*]. Listen …
an8.30:13.5: And what, *bhikkhus*, are the eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakkā*]?
an8.30:13.6: This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one of few wishes, not this *dhamma* for one of many wishes;
an8.30:13.7: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is content, not this *dhamma* for one who is discontent;
an8.30:13.8: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is secluded, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in company;
an8.30:13.9: this *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one whose energy is aroused, not this *dhamma* for one who is lazy;
an8.30:14.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one of few wishes, not this *dhamma* for one of many wishes,’
an8.30:14.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:14.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, being of few wishes, does not wish: ‘May they know me as one of few wishes’; being content, he does not wish: ‘May they know me as content’; being secluded, he does not wish: ‘May they know me as secluded’; being one whose energy is aroused, he does not wish: ‘May they know me as one whose energy is aroused’; being one with *sati* established, he does not wish: ‘May they know me as one with *sati* established’; being endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhito*], he does not wish: ‘May they know me as one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhito*]’; being one with discernment [*paññavā*], he does not wish: ‘May they know me as one with discernment [*paññavā*]’; being one who delights in non-proliferation, he does not wish: ‘May they know me as one who delights in non-proliferation.’
an8.30:14.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one of few wishes, not this *dhamma* for one of many wishes,’
an8.30:14.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:15.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is content, not this *dhamma* for one who is discontent,’ so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:15.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is content with any kind of robes, almsfood, lodging, and medicinal requisites for the sick.
an8.30:15.3: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is content, not this *dhamma* for one who is discontent,’
an8.30:15.4: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:16.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is secluded, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in company,’ so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:16.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, when a *bhikkhu* is dwelling secluded, *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, male lay followers, female lay followers, kings, royal ministers, sectarians, and disciples of sectarians become visitors to him.
an8.30:16.3: There the *bhikkhu*, with mind [*cittena*] inclining to seclusion [*viveka*], sloping to seclusion [*viveka*], leaning to seclusion [*viveka*], standing in seclusion [*viveka*], delighting in renunciation, becomes one who speaks only talk connected with sending them away.
an8.30:16.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who is secluded, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in company,’
an8.30:16.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:17.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one whose energy is aroused, not this *dhamma* for one who is lazy,’ so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:17.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells with energy aroused for the abandoning of unwholesome *dhammas* and for the attainment of wholesome *dhammas*; he is strong, firm in effort, not casting off the burden in wholesome *dhammas*.
an8.30:17.3: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one whose energy is aroused, not this *dhamma* for one who is lazy,’
an8.30:17.4: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:18.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with *sati* established, not this *dhamma* for one with *sati* lost,’
an8.30:18.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:18.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* has *sati* [*satimā*], endowed with supreme *sati* and skill, one who remembers and recollects what was done long ago and spoken long ago.
an8.30:18.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with *sati* established, not this *dhamma* for one with *sati* lost,’
an8.30:18.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:19.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not this *dhamma* for one without *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*],’
an8.30:19.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:19.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, secluded [*vivicca*] from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an8.30:19.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not this *dhamma* for one without *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*],’
an8.30:19.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having known with his mind [*cetasā*] the reflection in Venerable Anuruddha’s mind [*cetoparivitakkaṁ*] — just as a strong man might extend his bent arm or bend his extended arm; so too — disappeared from among the Bhaggas at Suṁsumāragira in the Bhesakaḷā Grove, the deer park, and appeared before Venerable Anuruddha among the Cedis, in the Eastern Bamboo Park.
an8.30:2.2: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat prepared.
an8.30:2.3: Venerable Anuruddha also bowed to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side.
an8.30:2.4: As Venerable Anuruddha was sitting to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to him:
an8.30:20.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with discernment [*paññavato*], not this *dhamma* for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*],’
an8.30:20.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:20.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* has discernment [*paññavā*], endowed with discernment [*paññāya*] of arising and passing away, noble, penetrative, rightly leading to the ending of *dukkha*.
an8.30:20.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one with discernment [*paññavato*], not this *dhamma* for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*],’
an8.30:20.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.
an8.30:21.1: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who is pleased by non-proliferation, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in proliferation, who is pleased by proliferation,’
an8.30:21.2: so it was said. And in reference to what was this said?
an8.30:21.3: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu’s* mind [*cittaṁ*] plunges into, becomes serene in, settles in, and is liberated in the cessation of proliferation.
an8.30:21.4: ‘This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who is pleased by non-proliferation, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in proliferation, who is pleased by proliferation,’
an8.30:21.5: so it was said, and in reference to this it was said.”
an8.30:22.1: Then Venerable Anuruddha dwelt for the next rains residence right there among the Cedis, in the Eastern Bamboo Park.
an8.30:22.2: Then Venerable Anuruddha, dwelling alone, withdrawn, heedful, ardent, and resolute, soon realized for himself with direct knowledge in this very life, entered, and dwelt in that unsurpassed end of the holy life for which sons of good family rightly go forth from the household life into homelessness.
an8.30:22.3: He understood: “Birth is destroyed, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.”
an8.30:22.4: And Venerable Anuruddha became one of the *arahants*.
an8.30:22.5: Then Venerable Anuruddha, having attained *arahantship*, at that time spoke these verses:
an8.30:23.2: the Teacher, unsurpassed in the world,
an8.30:25.2: I dwelt delighting in the teaching;
an8.30:25.3: The three true knowledges were attained,
an8.30:25.4: the Buddha’s teaching has been done.”
an8.30:25.6: The Householder Chapter is the third.
an8.30:26.1: Two with Ugga and two with Hatthaka,
an8.30:26.3: Two *balas*, the wrong times are spoken of,
an8.30:3.10: Therefore, Anuruddha, think [*vitakkehi*] this eighth great man’s thought [*mahāpurisavitakkaṁ*] too:
an8.30:3.11: ‘This *dhamma* is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who is pleased by non-proliferation, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in proliferation, who is pleased by proliferation.’
an8.30:3.2: It is good, Anuruddha, that you have thought this great man’s thought [*vitakkaṁ*]:
an8.30:3.3: ‘This *dhamma* is for one of few wishes, not this *dhamma* for one of many wishes;
an8.30:3.4: this *dhamma* is for one who is content, not this *dhamma* for one who is discontent;
an8.30:3.5: this *dhamma* is for one who is secluded, not this *dhamma* for one who delights in company;
an8.30:3.6: this *dhamma* is for one whose energy is aroused, not this *dhamma* for one who is lazy;
an8.30:3.7: this *dhamma* is for one with *sati* established, not this *dhamma* for one with *sati* lost;
an8.30:3.8: this *dhamma* is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not this *dhamma* for one without *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:3.9: this *dhamma* is for one with discernment [*paññavato*], not this *dhamma* for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*].’
an8.30:4.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, secluded [*vivicca*] from sensual pleasures, secluded [*vivicca*] from unwholesome *dhammas*, you will enter and dwell in the first *jhāna*, with thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha*.
an8.30:5.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the stilling of thoughts and examinations [*vitakkavicārānaṁ*], with internal confidence and unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], born of *samādhi*, with *pīti* and *sukha*, you will enter and dwell in the second *jhāna*.
an8.30:6.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the fading away of *pīti*, you will dwell equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*sato*] and fully aware [*sampajāno*], and you will experience *sukha* with the body, of which the noble ones declare: ‘Equanimous [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*satimā*], dwelling in *sukha*’; you will enter and dwell in the third *jhāna*.
an8.30:7.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the previous disappearance of joy and displeasure, neither *dukkha* nor *sukha*, with purity of *sati* by equanimity [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*], you will enter and dwell in the fourth *jhāna*.
an8.30:8.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], and you become one who gains at will, gains without trouble, gains without difficulty these four *jhānas* that are higher mind [*ābhicetasikānaṁ*], dwellings in *sukha* in the present life, then for you, Anuruddha, just as for a householder or a householder’s son there is a box full of cloths of many colors;
an8.30:8.2: so too your rag-robe will seem, as you dwell content, for delight, for non-agitation, for easeful dwelling, for descent into *nibbāna*.
an8.30:9.1: When, Anuruddha, you think [*vitakkessasi*] these eight great man’s thoughts [*mahāpurisavitakke*], and you become one who gains at will, gains without trouble, gains without difficulty these four *jhānas* that are higher mind [*ābhicetasikānaṁ*], dwellings in *sukha* in the present life, then for you, Anuruddha, just as for a householder or a householder’s son there is rice with selected dark grains, with many soups and many curries;
an8.30:9.2: so too your almsfood by the lump will seem, as you dwell content, for delight, for non-agitation, for easeful dwelling, for descent into *nibbāna*.
